name: Pasta e Fagioli
recipeIngredient: 2 small zucchini, sliced into half moons
or 5 Swiss chard leaves, tough stems removed and coarsely chopped
28 ounces plum tomatoes, diced with liquid (1 can)
freshly ground black pepper -- to taste
1 pound pasta, such as ziti, spirals, or shells
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Sauté the onion, carrots, and celery for a few minutes until softened.
3. Add the garlic, zucchini, and any dried herbs you are using to the pot.
4. Continue sautéing, stirring occasionally, for a few more minutes.
5. Add the spinach or chopped chard and cook until just wilted.
6. Mix in the diced tomatoes with liquid (or canned tomatoes) and cannellini beans.
7. Add water to reach your desired consistency. Adjust seasoning with parsley, fresh herbs, and black pepper.
8. Simmer gently for 15-20 minutes while you cook the pasta.
9. Cook the pasta al dente in a large pot of boiling salted water until done.
10. Drain the pasta and divide among individual bowls.
11. Top each serving with the bean soup mixture.
12. Optionally, sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese over the top and drizzle with olive oil before serving.
Note: Feel free to substitute or add your favorite fresh vegetables such as green or red bell peppers, summer squash, green beans, etc. Adding a chopped fresh tomato at the end of cooking is also a nice touch. Use fresh herbs whenever possible for maximum flavor.
